title: AI robots are learning to do simple human tasks at a factory in Massachusetts
description: Artificial intelligence works when computers learn from data that is readily available. But there is no data to teach robots to do simple human tasks. It needs to be created.
content:
BOSTON, Massachusetts (WBZ) — Artificial intelligence works when computers learn from data that is readily available. But there is no data to teach robots to do simple human tasks. It needs to be created.
That’s what MIT graduates Josh Gruenstein and... [2467 chars]

title: This new tool makes AI's role in student writing visible
description: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) has transformed college writing. As paper drafts are increasingly co-written with AI, professors are left wondering not whether students are using AI, but how. A 2025 AI in Education trend report found that 90% of college students use AI in their coursework, with nearly half using it during the drafting process. As AI becomes embedded in everyday writing, traditional tools like Grammarly or Turnitin for evaluating student learning fall short. If AI is to be expected in most student writing, then merely detecting its presence isn't enough.

title: AI rush meets workforce lag: Is readiness becoming the real crisis behind automation?
description: News News: There is a familiar myth surrounding artificial intelligence, that disruption will arrive dramatically, sweeping away jobs in a single, visible ruptur.

title: AI goldrush collides with voter backlash in Georgia
description: Multibillion-dollar artificial intelligence hubs are beginning to scramble politics in the crucial swing state as voters worry about costs and local control.
content:

title: Colorado lawmakers weigh youth chatbot protections, age attestation
description: Colorado lawmakers are grappling with regulation of artificial intelligence-powered chatbots and other protections for youth online as they also weigh privacy and freedom concerns.
content:
Once again, Colorado lawmakers say they are “trying to thread a really important needle” that’s eluded them in the past — establishing more online protections for young people, without running afoul of concerns over privacy and freedom.
